Allows you to read and safely modify the simulation state for the object. Use this instead of
if you need to directly change the body’s
or other physics properties. By default, it works in addition to the usual physics behavior, but
allows you to disable the default behavior and write custom force integration for a body.

Types that conform to VariantStorable can be stored in a Variant and can be extracted
back out of a Variant.
Sets the body’s total constant rotational forces applied during each physics update.
Returns the velocity vector at the body’s contact point.
Variant objects box various Godot Objects, you create them with one of the
constructors, and you can retrieve the contents using the various extension
constructors that are declared on the various types that are wrapped.
Applies a positioned impulse to the body.

Types that conform to VariantRepresentable can be stored directly in
with no conversion.  These include all of the Variant types from Godot (for

), Godot objects (those that subclass

Base class for all other classes in the engine.
